name: Sierra Morena and Collado Pisa Loop
name_original: Mestanza desde Puertollano – circular por Huertezuelas y la Sierra Morena
description: This difficult 88.3-mile road cycling route from Puertollano explores the Sierra Morena, offering challenging climbs and scenic views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Puertollano offers diverse landscapes, from volcanic formations in Campo de Calatrava to the mountainous terrain of Sierra Madrona and the Alcudia Valley. The region features a mix of challenging climbs and scenic routes, including paths through natural parks and past reservoirs. Road cyclists can explore varied elevation gains and distances across well-maintained surfaces. Best road cycling routes around Puertollano The most popular road cycling route is Puerto Madrona from Puertollano – circular via Mestanza and the Sierra Madrona, a difficult 72.2 miles (116.2 km) trail that takes 6 hours 16 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra Madrona. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ascent to the Castle of Calatrava la Nueva — circular route through the Sierra de Calatrava, a moderate 45.1 miles (72.7 km) path. This route includes an ascent to a historic castle within the Sierra de Calatrava. Local road cyclists also love the Almodóvar del Campo and Sierra de Cabez, a 43.4 miles (69.8 km) trail leading through Almodóvar del Campo and the Sierra de Cabez, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Puertollano is defined by volcanic landscapes, the Alcudia Valley, and the Sierra Madrona mountain range. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult climbs. The routes in Puertollano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. More than 693 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Puertollano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Puertollano?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Puertollano, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. The komoot community has contributed 28 routes in total, ensuring plenty of options for exploration.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Puertollano?
A: Road cycling in Puertollano offers diverse terrain. You'll find routes traversing volcanic landscapes in the Campo de Calatrava, scenic paths through the Alcudia Valley, and challenging climbs in the Sierra Madrona mountain range. The region provides a mix of well-maintained asphalt surfaces and varied elevation gains.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Puertollano offers several easier routes. For instance, the Vía Verde del Carbón y la Plata is an 11.8 km route following an old railway line, perfect for a more relaxed ride through the Ojailén valley. Overall, there are 6 easy routes available, providing gentle introductions to the region's cycling.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Puertollano?
A: Absolutely. Experienced riders can tackle routes with significant elevation gain and longer distances. A notable challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084847686/puerto-madrona-from-puertollano-circular-via-mestanza-and-the-sierra-madrona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Madrona from Puertollano – circular via Mestanza and the Sierra Madrona&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 116.2 km trail with substantial ascent through the Sierra Madrona. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084914119/sierra-morena-and-collado-pisa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena and Collado Pisa Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 142 km with nearly 2000 meters of climbing.

Q: What natural features can I see while road cycling in Puertollano?
A: The region boasts remarkable natural features. You can cycle through the unique volcanic landscapes of Campo de Calatrava, including the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt;. The Alcudia Valley offers picturesque pasturelands, and routes often pass by the Montoro and Tablillas Reservoirs. The Sierra Madrona Natural Park also provides stunning mountain scenery.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical and cultural points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246814191/ascent-to-the-castle-of-calatrava-la-nueva-circular-route-through-the-sierra-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castle of Calatrava la Nueva — circular route through the Sierra de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; leads directly to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Calatrava la Nueva and Sacred Convent&lt;/a&gt;. Puertollano's rich mining heritage is also evident, with sites like the Mining Museum and the Monument to the Miner in the city itself.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or highlights along the routes?
A: Many routes offer panoramic views, especially those climbing into the Sierra Madrona and Sierra Morena. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is a unique natural spectacle. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7199638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Agria&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7286202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Blanca&lt;/a&gt; are natural monuments worth seeing.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Puertollano?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Puertollano, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot, especially in July and August, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winters are typically mild but can have cooler days.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Puertollano?
A: For families or those seeking a gentler ride, the Vía Verde del Carbón y la Plata is an excellent choice. Its flat, old railway line path provides a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages. There are 6 easy routes in total, which are generally suitable for families.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Puertollano?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Puertollano are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246814191/ascent-to-the-castle-of-calatrava-la-nueva-circular-route-through-the-sierra-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castle of Calatrava la Nueva — circular route through the Sierra de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mestanza Village – Pantano del Montoro loop from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Puertollano?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from volcanic formations to mountainous terrain, and the variety of routes catering to different ability levels. The well-maintained surfaces and scenic views are frequently highlighted.

Q: Can I find parking near the starting points of road cycling routes in Puertollano?
A: While specific parking information for each route varies, Puertollano and its surrounding towns generally offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability in the starting village or town for your chosen route.
tips:
Settled on remains from the Bronze Age and a Visigothic settlement, this large fortress built in the 13th century is located on top of a hill.

It was built by the Order of Calatrava after the battle of Las Navas de Tolosa, becoming the headquarters of the order and one of the most important defensive fortresses in Castile.

More than a simple fortress, it is a complex that included a church, a convent, a hostel, a town and an external enclosure, all protected by an extensive wall.
You can see the fortified castle from miles away. You can understand the reasons why it was built on this dominant hill. Beautiful views of the defensive complex of the castle.
Shady area of the park where you can stop before the real climbing starts and fill up your jerry cans. Incredible views of the Fresneda river gorge.

At 700 meters above sea level, the small town of Huertezuelas is a cozy place to rest while ascending from the river along the CR-5041 highway. Rest and drink some more water because it is not the end of the climb.
Leaving Huertezuelas behind you still have 5km to go up to 1000 masl. The climb gains an elevation of almost 300m with an average gradient of 5.6%, so take your time to admire the incredible views.
At the foot of the Sierra Madrona, Mestanza is a small town south of Puertollano, in the Alcudia Valley. Have a drink at the fountain in the main square before embarking on the climbs ahead.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult road cycling route, requiring very good fitness. It's a substantial challenge due to its significant length of 142 kilometers and an elevation gain of over 1,940 meters. Expect demanding segments with significant climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect while cycling this route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road ride, the 'Hard' classification suggests demanding segments. You'll mostly find well-paved surfaces and quiet mountain roads, but given the Sierra Morena's varied terrain, there might be some ambiguity regarding unpaved sections. Be prepared for significant climbs and descents throughout.

faqs.question: What are the notable points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several interesting sights. You'll pass by the impressive Castle of Calatrava la Nueva and Sacred Convent, and enjoy a View of Castillo de Calatrava la Nueva. The charming settlements of Huertezuelas and Mestanza Village are also along the way, as is the Collado Pisa from Huertezuelas mountain pass.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Morena region is best enjoyed during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). These seasons offer milder temperatures, making the significant climbs and descents more manageable than in the intense summer heat. You'll also experience the region's natural beauty at its peak.

faqs.question: Are there any specific permits or entrance fees required for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It primarily follows public roads and paths through the Sierra Morena region.

faqs.question: Where can I park my vehicle to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts from Puertollano, you should look for public parking options within the town. Given the route's length and circular nature, finding secure parking near the starting point in Puertollano would be ideal.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails or paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll cycle along parts of [CIMA CR01] Collado Pisa, Palacio de Claveria. Castillo de Cva. la Nueva, Camino a Fontanar, Monumento Natural Carbonífero de Puertollano. Puertollano - Dehesa Boyal, and Cordel_alcoba.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through picturesque Mediterranean forests, meadows, and rolling hills, characterized by the dark tones of slate and quartzite. Keep an eye out for traditional 'Pueblos Blancos,' medieval castles, and olive groves. The region is a Biosphere Reserve, so you might spot wildlife such as vultures, eagles, and if you're very lucky, the elusive Iberian lynx.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation along the route, especially near Huertezuelas?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through settlements like Huertezuelas and Mestanza Village, where you can typically find local cafes, pubs, and potentially accommodation options. These villages offer opportunities to experience local culture and cuisine, including cheeses and cured hams.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness.' Its significant length and elevation gain make it unsuitable for families with young children or beginner cyclists. It's best suited for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenging ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas, this specific route is a long and challenging road cycling route. It's not designed for accompanying dogs, especially given the distance, elevation, and potential for traffic on roads. It's best to leave your canine companions at home for this particular adventure.
